name: Seafood Marinara Lisa Hlavaty Fdgn81a
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Dry thin whole wheat pasta
1/4 cup Chopped fresh parsley or 10 medium pitted black olives
1 pound Tuna or any other firm fish
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until al dente.
2. In a 2-quart microwave-safe casserole, combine olive oil, garlic, and onion. Microwave on high for 1 minute.
3. Add olives, undrained tomatoes, and tomato paste. Cover with wax paper and microwave on high for 3 to 5 minutes or until boiling.
4. Stir in mushrooms, thyme, basil, parsley, red pepper flakes, and pepper to taste. Add the tuna and cover again.
5. Microwave on high for 4 to 6 minutes or until the fish is cooked through.
6. Let stand for 5 minutes. Drain the pasta and divide among 4 bowls.
7. Spoon half of the seafood mixture over the pasta in each bowl.
